changes.citation: backend/scripts/validate_compile_manifest.py:89-143 — validate_compile_manifest() function contains no check for compile_run_id. knowledge_bus/schema/compile_manifest_schema_v1.yaml:42-43 — schema description states 'Alias compatible with ADR-RT-004; when present must equal compile_id.' Sprint scope item 8 requires Cursor to add this check. All existing compile manifests have compile_run_id == compile_id (verified: arch_rt3 line 2 and arch_rt5b_lipid_transport line 2 both match compile_id), so the check is non-breaking.

docs/audit-papers/active_intelligence_authority_manifest.md:11-18 — Wave 1 card evidence table shows only wave1_met_glycaemic_control as compiled; all other Wave 1 subsystems shown as hard-coded. This reflects ARCH-RT-3/ARCH-RT-5 state, not ARCH-RT-5B (which promoted all 7 subsystems). Root-cause/hypothesis table shows vitamin_d_low as shadow-only, not runtime-promoted (ARCH-RT-5C promoted it). Sprint deliverable list (prompt line 224) requires refreshing active_intelligence_authority_manifest.md. This is a required update, not optional.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:199-213 — scope items 1-13 define allowed surface: package manifest scan and classification, simple provenance resolution, provenance metadata additions where verifiable, hash refresh on launch-relevant manifests, compile_run_id == compile_id enforcement, schema strictness decision, estate index refresh, authority manifest refresh, provenance backfill audit, unresolved register. Out-of-scope list (lines 322-342) prohibits runtime behaviour changes, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, root-cause compiler, card evidence loader, frontend, PSI wiring, activation compiler, package regeneration, batch JSON spec extraction (unless bounded), clinical content.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:365-378 — 9 required test categories: compile_run_id == compile_id enforcement; launch-relevant manifests validate; manifest refs resolve from compiled artefacts; estate index resolves launch artefacts; package provenance classification output complete; no unclassified package; inferred not treated as explicit; kb52c packages classified correctly; five inferred card markers classified. Existing test: backend/tests/unit/test_compile_manifest_schema_v1.py:58-79 — covers schema load and minimal pilot manifest acceptance only. All 9 required test categories are new coverage.

Risk 1: compile manifest hash refresh — computed SHA-256 hashes are deterministic from file bytes at sprint time but will become stale if files change post-sprint. This is acceptable for a provenance audit sprint; hashes serve as evidence-at-time-of-audit, not live assertions. Risk 2: validate_compile_manifest.py tightening — adding compile_run_id == compile_id check is non-breaking against existing manifests (all 8 have matching values). Risk 3: schema enum extension — adding health_system_card_evidence to output_type enum is additive/non-breaking. No Intelligence Core files are modified; runtime behaviour cannot change from this sprint's scope.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:380-394 — 11 STOP conditions covering: missing authority files (#1), package manifest scan failure (#2), unclassifiable package (#3), explicit/inferred indistinguishable (#4), kb52c extraction requires compiler (#5), hash refresh unclassifiable (#6), schema changes break launch-relevant manifests (#7), package changes alter runtime signal behaviour (#8), package clinical content modification required (#9), scope expands into runtime surfaces (#10), tests cannot prove completeness (#11).

knowledge_bus/schema/compile_manifest_schema_v1.yaml:172-183 — output_type enum contains: signal_library, research_brief, package_manifest, promoted_signal_intelligence, hypothesis_artefact, card_evidence_artefact, compile_estate_index. BUT: all 7 card evidence compile manifests (arch_rt3_glycaemic_card_evidence.yaml:13; arch_rt5b_lipid_transport_card_evidence.yaml:25 and all other arch_rt5b_* manifests) use output_type: health_system_card_evidence — which is NOT in the schema enum. The validator currently does not enforce output_type against the schema enum for non-activation compile_modes, so existing manifests PASS. If Cursor tightens the validator to enforce output_type enum strictly without first adding health_system_card_evidence to the enum, ALL 7 card evidence manifests will fail validation — triggering STOP condition 7 ('manifest validator changes would break existing launch-relevant manifests'). CURSOR MUST: (1) add health_system_card_evidence to the schema output_type enum, (2) then tighten the validator, (3) confirm all 8 manifests still pass. The schema extension is non-breaking (additive).

backend/scripts/validate_compile_manifest.py:89-143 — validate_compile_manifest() has no compile_run_id check. knowledge_bus/schema/compile_manifest_schema_v1.yaml:42-43 — schema description requires equality when both present. Sprint scope item 8 (prompt line 208) requires this enforcement. Implementation: in validate_compile_manifest(), after extracting compile_id, check if compile_run_id is present; if so, assert equality and add an error if they differ. All 8 existing compile manifests have matching values (arch_rt3: compile_id == compile_run_id == 'arch-rt-3-glycaemic-card-evidence-001'; arch_rt5b_lipid_transport: both equal 'arch-rt-5b-lipid-transport-card-evidence-001') — change is non-breaking.

All 8 launch-relevant compile manifests have source_hash: pending_inventory_refresh and output_hash: pending_inventory_refresh (confirmed: arch_rt5b_lipid_transport_card_evidence.yaml:10,14,18,22,28; arch_rt3_glycaemic_card_evidence.yaml:9,14). Sprint scope item 7 (prompt line 207) requires hash refresh where source/output files are stable. Cursor must compute SHA-256 hashes for each referenced source_path and output_path file. Referenced source files (investigation specs) and output files (card evidence artefacts, hypothesis artefact) are confirmed present. If any file is not found, Cursor must STOP on that manifest and classify the gap rather than leaving pending_inventory_refresh in place. arch_rt4_vitamin_d_hypothesis.yaml was also using pending_inventory_refresh hashes (confirmed from prior audit).

docs/audit-papers/active_intelligence_authority_manifest.md:11 — Wave 1 card evidence table lists only wave1_met_glycaemic_control as compiled; other 6 subsystems shown as hard-coded. ARCH-RT-5B promoted all 7 subsystems (estate_index_v1.yaml confirms 7 compiled card artefacts). active_intelligence_authority_manifest.md:16-18 — root-cause table shows vitamin_d_low as shadow-only; ARCH-RT-5C promoted it to runtime. This document is a required deliverable (prompt line 224) and must be updated to reflect the post-ARCH-RT-5C authority state. Required updates: (1) expand Wave 1 card evidence table to show all 7 compiled subsystems; (2) update root-cause/hypothesis table to show signal_vitamin_d_low as runtime_promoted_compiled authority.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:322-342 — out-of-scope prohibits: runtime behaviour changes, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, root-cause compiler runtime logic, card evidence loader runtime logic, frontend, PSI runtime wiring, activation compiler implementation, package regeneration, batch JSON spec extraction (unless hardening confirms bounded scope), clinical thresholds, scoring rails, biomarker SSOT, investigation specs, PSI artefacts, root-cause YAML, card evidence clinical content, fallback parsers.

compile_manifest_schema_v1.yaml is the sole schema authority. validate_compile_manifest.py is the sole validator. estate_index_v1.yaml is the sole estate ledger. package_generation_inventory.md is the sole package-count authority. No duplicate sources exist. Sprint must not create duplicate schema files or parallel validators.
